title: ephedra fragilis subsp. campylopoda
intro: Ephedra fragilis subsp. campylopoda is a plant species within the Ephedraceae family, commonly known as joint pine or desert ephedra. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include stimulating the nervous system, increasing circulation, and aiding in respiratory issues. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing symptoms of bronchitis, asthma, and allergies, as well as providing a boost to the immune system. Ephedra fragilis subsp. campylopoda is often prepared for medicinal use in herbal teas, capsules, or extracts.
